NKJV.1: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy, To the church of the Thessalonians in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
NKJV.2: We give thanks to God always for you all, making mention of you in our prayers,
NKJV.3: remembering without ceasing your work of faith, labor of love, and patience of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ in the sight of our God and Father,
NKJV.4: knowing, beloved brethren, your election by God.
NKJV.5: For our gospel did not come to you in word only, but also in power, and in the Holy Spirit and in much assurance, as you know what kind of men we were among you for your sake.
NKJV.6: And you became followers of us and of the Lord, having received the word in much affliction, with joy of the Holy Spirit,
NKJV.7: so that you became examples to all in Macedonia and Achaia who believe.
NKJV.8: For from you the word of the Lord has sounded forth, not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but also in every place. Your faith toward God has gone out, so that we do not need to say anything.
NKJV.9: For they themselves declare concerning us what manner of entry we had to you, and how you turned to God from idols to serve the living and true God,
NKJV.10: and to wait for His Son from heaven, whom He raised from the dead, even Jesus who delivers us from the wrath to come.

ESV.1: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy, To the church of the Thessalonians in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace to you and peace.
ESV.2: We give thanks to God always for all of you, constantly  mentioning you in our prayers,
ESV.3: remembering before our God and Father your work of faith and labor of love and steadfastness of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ.
ESV.4: For we know, brothers  loved by God, that he has chosen you,
ESV.5: because our gospel came to you not only in word, but also in power and in the Holy Spirit and with full conviction. You know what kind of men we proved to be among you for your sake.
ESV.6: And you became imitators of us and of the Lord, for you received the word in much affliction, with the joy of the Holy Spirit,
ESV.7: so that you became an example to all the believers in Macedonia and in Achaia.
ESV.8: For not only has the word of the Lord sounded forth from you in Macedonia and Achaia, but your faith in God has gone forth everywhere, so that we need not say anything.
ESV.9: For they themselves report concerning us the kind of reception we had among you, and how you turned to God from idols to serve the living and true God,
ESV.10: and to wait for his Son from heaven, whom he raised from the dead, Jesus who delivers us from the wrath to come.

NASB.1: Paul and Silvanus and Timothy, To the church of the Thessalonians in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace to you and peace.
NASB.2: We give thanks to God always for all of you, making mention [of you] in our prayers;
NASB.3: constantly bearing in mind your work of faith and labor of love and steadfastness of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ in the presence of our God and Father,
NASB.4: knowing, brethren beloved by God, [His] choice of you;
NASB.5: for our gospel did not come to you in word only, but also in power and in the Holy Spirit and with full conviction; just as you know what kind of men we proved to be among you for your sake.
NASB.6: You also became imitators of us and of the Lord, having received the word in much tribulation with the joy of the Holy Spirit,
NASB.7: so that you became an example to all the believers in Macedonia and in Achaia.
NASB.8: For the word of the Lord has sounded forth from you, not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but also in every place your faith toward God has gone forth, so that we have no need to say anything.
NASB.9: For they themselves report about us what kind of a reception we had with you, and how you turned to God from idols to serve a living and true God,
NASB.10: and to wait for His Son from heaven, whom He raised from the dead, [that is] Jesus, who rescues us from the wrath to come.

NLT.1: This letter is from Paul, Silas, and Timothy. We are writing to the church in Thessalonica, to you who belong to God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. May God give you grace and peace.
NLT.2: We always thank God for all of you and pray for you constantly.
NLT.3: As we pray to our God and Father about you, we think of your faithful work, your loving deeds, and the enduring hope you have because of our Lord Jesus Christ.
NLT.4: We know, dear brothers and sisters, that God loves you and has chosen you to be his own people.
NLT.5: For when we brought you the Good News, it was not only with words but also with power, for the Holy Spirit gave you full assurance that what we said was true. And you know of our concern for you from the way we lived when we were with you.
NLT.6: So you received the message with joy from the Holy Spirit in spite of the severe suffering it brought you. In this way, you imitated both us and the Lord.
NLT.7: As a result, you have become an example to all the believers in Greece — throughout both Macedonia and Achaia.
NLT.8: And now the word of the Lord is ringing out from you to people everywhere, even beyond Macedonia and Achaia, for wherever we go we find people telling us about your faith in God. We don’t need to tell them about it,
NLT.9: for they keep talking about the wonderful welcome you gave us and how you turned away from idols to serve the living and true God.
NLT.10: And they speak of how you are looking forward to the coming of God’s Son from heaven — Jesus, whom God raised from the dead. He is the one who has rescued us from the terrors of the coming judgment.

WEB.1: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy, to the assembly of the Thessalonians in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
WEB.2: We always give thanks to God for all of you, mentioning you in our prayers,
WEB.3: remembering without ceasing your work of faith and labor of love and perseverance of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ, before our God and Father.
WEB.4: We know, brothers  loved by God, that you are chosen,
WEB.5: and that our Good News came to you not in word only, but also in power, and in the Holy Spirit, and with much assurance. You know what kind of men we showed ourselves to be among you for your sake.
WEB.6: You became imitators of us, and of the Lord, having received the word in much affliction, with joy of the Holy Spirit,
WEB.7: so that you became an example to all who believe in Macedonia and in Achaia.
WEB.8: For from you the word of the Lord has been declared, not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but also in every place your faith toward God has gone out; so that we need not to say anything.
WEB.9: For they themselves report concerning us what kind of a reception we had from you; and how you turned to God from idols, to serve a living and true God,
WEB.10: and to wait for his Son from heaven, whom he raised from the dead — Jesus, who delivers us from the wrath to come.

CSB17.1: Paul, Silvanus, <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓚ</sup> and Timothy: <sup>ⓛ</sup> To the church of the Thessalonians <sup>ⓜ</sup> in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. Grace to you and peace. <sup>[2]</sup>
CSB17.2: We always thank God for all of you, making mention of you constantly in our prayers.
CSB17.3: We recall, in the presence of our God and Father, <sup>ⓝ</sup> your work produced by faith, <sup>ⓐ</sup> your labor motivated by love, and your endurance inspired by hope in our Lord Jesus Christ. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.4: For we know, brothers and sisters loved <sup>ⓒ</sup> by God, <sup>ⓓ</sup> that he has chosen you, <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.5: because our gospel did not come to you in word only, but also in power, in the Holy Spirit, <sup>ⓕ</sup> and with full assurance. You know how we lived among you <sup>ⓖ</sup> for your benefit,
CSB17.6: and you yourselves became imitators of us and of the Lord when, in spite of severe persecution, you welcomed the message <sup>ⓗ</sup> with joy from the Holy Spirit.
CSB17.7: As a result, you became an example to all the believers in Macedonia <sup>ⓘ</sup> and Achaia. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.8: For the word of the Lord rang out from you, not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but in every place that your faith <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓚ</sup> in God has gone out. Therefore, we don’t need to say anything,
CSB17.9: for they themselves report <sup>[4]</sup> what kind of reception <sup>ⓛ</sup> we had from you: how you turned <sup>ⓜ</sup> to God from idols <sup>ⓝ</sup> to serve the living <sup>ⓐ</sup> and true <sup>ⓑ</sup> God
CSB17.10: and to wait <sup>ⓒ</sup> for his Son <sup>ⓓ</sup> from heaven, whom he raised from the dead <sup>ⓔ</sup>  — Jesus, who rescues us <sup>ⓕ</sup> from the coming wrath. <sup>ⓖ</sup>

AMP.1:
Paul, Silvanus (Silas), and Timothy,
To the church of the Thessalonians in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace to you and peace [inner calm and spiritual well-being from God].
AMP.2: We give thanks to God always for all of you, continually mentioning you in our prayers;
AMP.3: recalling unceasingly before our God and Father your work energized by faith, and your service motivated by love and unwavering hope in [the return of] our Lord Jesus Christ.
AMP.4: Brothers and sisters beloved by God, we know that He has chosen you;
AMP.5: for our good news [regarding salvation] came to you not only in word, but also in [its inherent] power and in the Holy Spirit and with great conviction [on our part]. You know what kind of men we proved to be among you for your benefit.
AMP.6: You became imitators of us and [through us] of the Lord, after you welcomed our message in [a time of] great trouble with the joy supplied by the Holy Spirit;
AMP.7: so that you became an example to all the believers in Macedonia and in Achaia.
AMP.8: For the word of the Lord has resounded from you and has echoed [like thunder], not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but in every place [the news of] your [great] faith in God has spread, so that we never need to say anything about it.
AMP.9: For they themselves report about us, telling what kind of reception we had among you, and how you turned to God from idols to serve the living and true God,
AMP.10: and to [look forward and confidently] wait for [the coming of] His Son from heaven, whom He raised from the dead—Jesus, who [personally] rescues us from the coming wrath [and draws us to Himself, granting us all the privileges and rewards of a new life with Him].

MSG.1: I, Paul, together here with Silas and Timothy, send greetings to the church at Thessalonica, Christians assembled by God the Father and by the Master, Jesus Christ. God's amazing grace be with you! God's robust peace!
MSG.2: Every time we think of you, we thank God for you. Day and night you're in our prayers
MSG.3: as we call to mind your work of faith, your labor of love, and your patience of hope in following our Master, Jesus Christ, before God our Father.
MSG.4: It is clear to us, friends, that God not only loves you very much but also has put his hand on you for something special.
MSG.5: When the Message we preached came to you, it wasn't just words. Something happened in you. The Holy Spirit put steel in your convictions. You paid careful attention to the way we lived among you,
MSG.6: and determined to live that way yourselves. In imitating us, you imitated the Master. Although great trouble accompanied the Word, you were able to take great joy from the Holy Spirit!—taking the trouble with the joy, the joy with the trouble.
MSG.7: Do you know that all over the provinces of both Macedonia and Achaia believers look up to you?
MSG.8: The word has gotten around. Your lives are echoing the Master's Word, not only in the provinces but all over the place. The news of your faith in God is out. We don't even have to say anything anymore—you're the message!
MSG.9: People come up and tell us how you received us with open arms, how you deserted the dead idols of your old life so you could embrace and serve God, the true God.
MSG.10: They marvel at how expectantly you await the arrival of his Son, whom he raised from the dead—Jesus, who rescued us from certain doom.

NIV.1:
Paul, Silas and Timothy,
To the church of the Thessalonians in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ:
Grace and peace to you.
NIV.2:
Thanksgiving for the Thessalonians’ Faith
We always thank God for all of you, mentioning you in our prayers.
NIV.3: We continually remember before our God and Father your work produced by faith, your labor prompted by love, and your endurance inspired by hope in our Lord Jesus Christ.
NIV.4: For we know, brothers loved by God, that he has chosen you,
NIV.5: because our gospel came to you not simply with words, but also with power, with the Holy Spirit and with deep conviction. You know how we lived among you for your sake.
NIV.6: You became imitators of us and of the Lord; in spite of severe suffering, you welcomed the message with the joy given by the Holy Spirit.
NIV.7: And so you became a model to all the believers in Macedonia and Achaia.
NIV.8: The Lord’s message rang out from you not only in Macedonia and Achaia—your faith in God has become known everywhere. Therefore we do not need to say anything about it,
NIV.9: for they themselves report what kind of reception you gave us. They tell how you turned to God from idols to serve the living and true God,
NIV.10: and to wait for his Son from heaven, whom he raised from the dead—Jesus, who rescues us from the coming wrath.

NRSVCE.1: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy, To the church of the Thessalonians in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace to you and peace.
NRSVCE.2: We always give thanks to God for all of you and mention you in our prayers, constantly
NRSVCE.3: remembering before our God and Father your work of faith and labor of love and steadfastness of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.4: For we know, brothers and sisters beloved by God, that he has chosen you,
NRSVCE.5: because our message of the gospel came to you not in word only, but also in power and in the Holy Spirit and with full conviction; just as you know what kind of persons we proved to be among you for your sake.
NRSVCE.6: And you became imitators of us and of the Lord, for in spite of persecution you received the word with joy inspired by the Holy Spirit,
NRSVCE.7: so that you became an example to all the believers in Macedonia and in Achaia.
NRSVCE.8: For the word of the Lord has sounded forth from you not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but in every place your faith in God has become known, so that we have no need to speak about it.
NRSVCE.9: For the people of those regions report about us what kind of welcome we had among you, and how you turned to God from idols, to serve a living and true God,
NRSVCE.10: and to wait for his Son from heaven, whom he raised from the dead — Jesus, who rescues us from the wrath that is coming.

NET.1: From Paul and Silvanus and Timothy, to the church of the Thessalonians in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. Grace and peace to you!
NET.2: We thank God always for all of you as we mention you constantly in our prayers,
NET.3: because we recall in the presence of our God and Father your work of faith and labor of love and endurance of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ.
NET.4: We know, brothers and sisters loved by God, that he has chosen you,
NET.5: in that our gospel did not come to you merely in words, but in power and in the Holy Spirit and with deep conviction (surely you recall the character we displayed when we came among you to help you).
NET.6: And you became imitators of us and of the Lord, when you received the message with joy that comes from the Holy Spirit, despite great affliction.
NET.7: As a result you became an example to all the believers in Macedonia and in Achaia.
NET.8: For from you the message of the Lord has echoed forth not just in Macedonia and Achaia, but in every place reports of your faith in God have spread, so that we do not need to say anything.
NET.9: For people everywhere report how you welcomed us and how you turned to God from idols to serve the living and true God
NET.10: and to wait for his Son from heaven, whom he raised from the dead, Jesus our deliverer from the coming wrath.
